replies.author: @Stakex
replies.text:
She got the job because Lucas wanted her to have the job, and that was right at the beginning of the "girl power" era in Hollywood so having a female studio head was a big PR win for Disney among people that care about that stuff. Why she still has the job despite all the failures at LF over the years is up for debate and we may never learn everything that went on behind the scenes, but I suspect it's a combination of her simply refusing to leave while Iger didn't want to be seen as strong arming a well-connected woman out the door while the female empowerment era was still in full swing. KK has also installed a lot of loyalist at LF that could have caused a lot of issues if her departure wasn't amicable. 

That said, I do think the winds of change are in the air more than people think at LF and have been for a while. It seems pretty obvious there was a change of course sometime in late 2024 when out of nowhere the Starfighter rumors started. It wasn't some far flung movie that MIGHT come out in five years... it went from rumor to finished shooting in a year, which has to be a record for LF in the Disney era. Not only that, but the Rey movie was supposed to be the next film after Mando and Grogu but Starfighter has taken its spot. I also don't think it's a coincidence that Starfighter has a bankable straight white male lead in Gosling instead of Riddley and a box office proven director in Shawn Leavy, as opposed to an activist that's never made a Hollywood film before. The Starfighter reveal also came along with the reveal that Disney is actively working on a new trilogy that will basically be Episode 10-12 (even if they're not called that), something KK seemed opposed to in the past.

Simply put, I think KK has more or less been sidelined for a while now. I suspect the constant failure of "girl power" projects at both LF and Marvel (including several high-profile failures in 23 and 24), and the smashing success of Deadpool and Wolverine is the nexus point where Iger and Disney upper management finally decided to get serious about making money again. Perhaps KK is on board with that, perhaps not. It's clear however her vision for Star Wars in 2023, which was based around the Rey movie, is not where the brand is heading today.

replies.author: @TankHunter678
replies.text:
@PrinceIsot The studio only gets about half of the box office revenue because the rest of the box office has to go to the theaters themselves and in the case of china the theater and the CCP (so you only get ~25% of the box office in china going to the studio), then after that you have to factor in the production cost and advertising cost. This is why a movie has to make a box office return of 2.5-3x the production cost to be considered breaking even as advertising is generally equal to the production cost. Only after it breaks even do you get a monetary return.

Do the same for all the other projects that the studio puts out.

After that you also need to take into account the money lost by other project failures which draws from the same pool of funds for the studio, including the TV/D+ stuff where viewership has a direct influence on advertising revenue. Each failed project adds tremendous amounts to the debt.

The only way the math you talk about works of Disney getting their 4b back from the box office is if one assumes the studio gets 100% of the box office and the production/advertising costs are 0. Reality simply does not work that way.

replies.author: @warlawds7007
replies.text: I'm not sure I get ALL the Filoni hate, other than being a YES man for Kennedy.  But honestly, he probably wasn't going to be employed very long if he was creatively pushing back constantly at KK. His main directing/writing credits are a mixed bag.  The Clone Wars series is easily his best work, which successfully "explained" the nuance of the prequels, to the point that they have much more emotional impact. Rebels, while not quite as good, is still some pretty decent content.  His strength is definitely with animated shows.  He did Mandalorian, which is both good and bad, more-so as it went on. The Obi-Wan show, Boba-Fett and Ahsoka shows were all pretty bad though.  Most of the other turd content Lucasfilm has put out, he wasn't much more than a consultant.  So outside of Tony Gilroy, who's the major creative of Rogue One and Andor, Filoni is the one Disney creative that at least has SOME good Star Wars content on his resume.

Because based on his original work, and being hand-picked by Lucas I'd say he has my vote. Time will tell if the content released after her exit stays the same or improves. That Clone Wars finale is such good SW content though. Rebels had a little more filler but some seriously good moments all throughout.
